engineGuide.h2: Range Rover Evoque Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Range Rover Evoque across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.2L Diesel
entries.history: The PSA DW12 engine powered first-generation Evoques, replacing Ford’s DW10 with a stronger bottom end and 16-valve head for improved refinement and torque delivery.
compatibleVariants: eD4, eD4 Black, TD4, TD4 Pure, TD4 SE, SD4, SD4 Dynamic, SD4 Prestige
commonFailures: Timing chain stretch causing rattle on startup (often from 70,000 miles)
Turbocharger bearing failure, whistling and power loss
Injector seal leaks leading to diesel in oil and sump dilution
entries.cta: Get quotes for DW12 Range Rover Evoque engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: The first Ingenium diesel introduced JLR’s modular engine architecture with ultra-low friction coatings and a variable-geometry turbo, designed for Euro 6 compliance and improved fuel economy.
compatibleVariants: TD4 Ingenium, TD4 Ingenium SE, SD4 Ingenium, SD4 Ingenium HSE Dynamic
commonFailures: Oil dilution from incomplete DPF regeneration, leading to timing chain wear
Timing chain tensioner failure causing chain rattle and potential engine seizure
High-pressure fuel pump failure, difficult starting and lack of power
entries.cta: Get quotes for AJ200D Range Rover Evoque engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: A lower-output calibration of the Ingenium diesel, fitted primarily to front-wheel-drive eD4 variants, optimised for reduced CO2 emissions and tax band efficiency.
commonFailures: Camshaft bearing wear accelerated by oil dilution from DPF issues
Turbocharger failure, often from contaminated oil supply
entries.cta: Get quotes for 204DTD Range Rover Evoque engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel / MHEV Diesel
entries.history: The second-generation Ingenium diesel introduced mild hybrid technology with a belt-integrated starter generator, improving stop-start refinement and reducing turbo lag.
compatibleVariants: D165, D165 S, D165 SE, D165 R-Dynamic, D200, D200 R-Dynamic HSE
commonFailures: DPF regeneration distance issues due to exhaust system placement, causing oil dilution
Timing chain tensioner concerns on earlier builds (pre-November 2019)
Diesel particulate filter blockage from urban-only driving patterns
entries.cta: Get quotes for AJ20D4 Range Rover Evoque engine replacement

engineGuide.closing: Can't find your engine code? Enter your registration number above - we'll instantly identify the exact unit, its technical specifications, and live replacement prices from UK Range Rover Evoque engine specialists. Every rebuilt engine comes with a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty.

commonProblems.h2: Range Rover Evoque Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Range Rover Evoque’s engine lineup spans the robust PSA DW12 diesel (2011-2015) and the more problematic Ingenium diesel family (2015-present), which dominates replacement enquiries for timing chain failure and oil dilution. Petrol variants, particularly the Si4 and later Ingenium P-series, have fewer systemic failures but still suffer turbo and high-pressure fuel pump issues at higher mileages. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Below, we break down the most common Evoque engine failures, what they cost to fix at a dealer versus a specialist, and when replacement is the only sensible choice.

problems.group: Range Rover Evoque Ingenium Timing Chain Failure
problems.h4: Range Rover Evoque Ingenium Timing Chain Failure - Why AJ200D and 204DTA Engines Fail Prematurely

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Timing Chain & Tensioner Replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace timing chain kit (chain, tensioner, guides, cam sprockets), oil pump, and perform full oil system flush.
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if caught before chain jump. Adds 60k+ miles if upgraded parts used.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Chain Failure with Valve Damage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Strip cylinder head, replace damaged valves and lifters, full timing chain kit, head gasket set, oil flush.
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for engines with low-mileage bottom end but minor valve contact.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded timing chain components, new oil pump, injectors recalibrated, bench tested, 12-month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: Best value for high-mileage engines (90k+). Addresses all failure points permanently.

problems.recommendation: If your Ingenium diesel has timing chain rattle on cold start but still runs smoothly, invest in a preventative chain replacement at a specialist (£1,200-£1,800). This is significantly cheaper than a full rebuild and, with upgraded components, will outlast the original. However, if the engine has already jumped timing or lost oil pressure, skip the intermediate repair on a high-mileage Evoque - a rebuilt unit with warranty (£3,500-£5,500) offers better long-term value than repairing a compromised bottom end.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt AJ200D engine prices for your Range Rover Evoque

problems.h4: Range Rover Evoque Oil Dilution - The Ingenium Diesel Engine Killer

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Oil Dilution Mitigation (Flush & Software Update)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full oil and filter change (two flushes), DPF static regeneration, PCM software update to modify regen strategy.
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix if underlying driving pattern doesn't change. May last 10-20k miles before recurrence.

repairOptions.tier: Major - Oil Dilution Damage (Bottom End Failure)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace oil pump, timing chain kit, main and big-end bearings, oil cooler, full system flush.
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if engine hasn't seized. High-risk repair - damage often extends beyond bearings.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with revised DPF calibration, upgraded oil pump, injector coding, 12-month warranty.
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if engine has run with heavily diluted oil for extended period or has seized.

problems.recommendation: Oil dilution is a design flaw in the early Ingenium diesel calibration. If your oil level warning appears or the dipstick shows rising level, act immediately: a specialist oil flush and software update (£250-£400) can prevent catastrophic damage. However, if your Evoque has already suffered bottom end knocking or seized, do not repair - fit a rebuilt unit with the latest engine management calibration (£3,500-£5,500). On a car worth £6,000-£12,000, replacement is financially sensible.
problems.cta: Get quotes for a rebuilt Ingenium diesel engine with upgraded oil system components

problems.group: Range Rover Evoque 2.2 DW12 Turbo Failure
problems.h4: Range Rover Evoque 2.2 DW12 Turbo Failure - The High-Mileage Diesel Weak Point

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Turbo Clean & VGT Actuator Replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove turbo, decarbonise VGT mechanism, replace actuator, new oil feed pipe and gaskets.
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for early-stage sticking (intermittent limp mode). Adds 30-40k miles.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace turbocharger (new or OE-quality reman), oil feed and return lines, oil change, new gaskets.
repairOptions.longevity: Best for failed turbo bearings or seized VGT. Expect 80k+ miles from quality replacement.

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Engine with Turbo (Debris Risk)
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit used or rebuilt engine with new turbo, full oil system flush to remove metal debris.
repairOptions.longevity: Essential if turbo failure sent metal fragments into oil galleries. Prevents secondary bearing failure.

problems.recommendation: The DW12 engine itself is robust, but turbo failure at high mileage is common. If caught early with intermittent boost issues, a specialist turbo clean and actuator replacement (£450-700) is cost-effective. However, if the turbo has already failed catastrophically (smoke, whining, loss of power), do not simply replace the turbo - metal debris often contaminates oil galleries. On a 2011-2015 Evoque worth £4,000-£8,000, a specialist turbo replacement (£1,000-£1,600) is the sweet spot if no debris has circulated.
problems.cta: Compare DW12 turbo replacement and rebuilt engine prices for your Evoque

problems.group: Range Rover Evoque Si4 & P250 High-Pressure Fuel Pump Failure
problems.h4: Range Rover Evoque Si4 & P250 High-Pressure Fuel Pump Failure - Petrol Engine Cut-Out Risk

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace HPFP, fuel filter, perform fuel system flush, reset adaptation values.
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable if caught immediately after first starting issues or power loss.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace HPFP, all four injectors, fuel rail pressure sensor, full fuel system flush and coding.
repairOptions.longevity: Required if metal debris has reached injectors (evidenced by misfire codes).

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Fuel System & Engine Inspection
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace HPFP, injectors, fuel rail, low-pressure pump, flush fuel tank, inspect cylinder bore scoring.
repairOptions.longevity: Necessary for engines that have run lean due to fuel starvation - bore scoring is common after HPFP failure.

problems.recommendation: HPFP failure on the PT204 petrol engine is less common than on diesel Ingeniums, but it does occur and can be costly. If your Evoque Si4 or P250 hesitates on acceleration or cuts out, replace the HPFP immediately at a specialist (£600-900) before debris spreads. Once you have injector codes or misfires, the repair cost jumps significantly. On high-mileage examples (90k+), if the engine has suffered bore scoring from lean running, replacement is the only safe option - a used engine with warranty typically runs £2,000-£3,500 plus fitting.
problems.cta: Get quotes for PT204 petrol engine replacement and HPFP repair specialist costs

problems.group: Range Rover Evoque DPF Regeneration Issues
problems.h4: Range Rover Evoque DPF Regeneration Issues - The Urban Driving Diesel Problem

repairOptions.tier: Minor - DPF Static Regeneration & Oil Change
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Forced DPF regen using diagnostic equipment, full oil and filter change, reset learned values.
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable if DPF soot load is below 80% and no permanent damage. Buys 10-15k miles if driving pattern improves.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove DPF, pressure-clean and chemically dissolve ash deposits, replace differential pressure sensors, oil change.
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for partially blocked DPF (soot load 80-100%). Adds 30-40k miles.

repairOptions.tier: Major - DPF Replacement + Oil Dilution Damage
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace DPF with new or OE-grade unit, full oil system flush, replace timing chain and tensioner if oil degradation evident.
repairOptions.longevity: Required if DPF is cracked or irreversibly blocked. Often accompanied by oil dilution damage - inspect timing components.

problems.recommendation: If your Evoque is used mainly for school runs and shopping trips, a diesel is the wrong tool. For owners committed to keeping their Ingenium diesel, a specialist static regeneration and oil change (£200-350) every 15,000 miles is a worthwhile preventative cost. However, if you have repeated DPF warnings or rising oil levels, replace the Evoque with a petrol or hybrid - fitting a replacement DPF to a car that will never see a motorway is throwing money away. For those who do mix town and motorway driving, a specialist DPF clean (£400-650) followed by regular monthly motorway runs will prevent recurrence.
problems.cta: Compare DPF repair and Ingenium engine replacement costs for your Evoque

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Range Rover Evoque
finalCta.paragraph: Timing chain failure, oil dilution, turbo failure, HPFP issues - each of these common Evoque engine problems can feel like the end of the road. But with EngineMarket, comparing prices from trusted UK Range Rover Evoque specialists is simple and free. Every rebuilt engine supplied through our network carries upgraded components designed to prevent the original failure, a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty, and nationwide delivery or fitting options. Don't pay main dealer premiums for an engine replacement your Evoque doesn't need - compare specialist prices and get back on the road with confidence.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Range Rover Evoque Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Range Rover Evoque and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineTypes.h2: Range Rover Evoque Engine Types - Options, Costs & What to Expect
engineTypes.intro: Every engine type for the Range Rover Evoque is available through our UK network, with typical price ranges varying by variant, engine code (DW12, AJ200D, PT204, AJ20D4) and your specific budget.

types.title: Range Rover Evoque Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: Factory-spec engine rebuilt using OEM-grade components, shot-blasted block, new piston rings and bearings.
types.frontDescription: Factory-spec engine rebuilt using OEM-grade components, shot-blasted block, new piston rings and bearings.
types.frontDisclaimer: Based on AJ200D and DW12 core pricing.
types.backDescription: A reconditioned Evoque engine is stripped to bare block, ultrasonically cleaned, machined to factory tolerances and reassembled with new timing chains, gaskets and seals.
backBullets: Ideal for Ingenium diesel engines with oil dilution damage
All AJ200D and AJ20D4 units include upgraded timing chain tensioners
Lead time typically 5-10 working days with 12-24 month warranty
types.cta: Compare reconditioned Range Rover Evoque engine prices

types.title: Range Rover Evoque Rebuilt Engines
types.description: Focused rebuild replacing worn components (timing chain, bearings, piston rings) without full block machining.
types.frontDescription: Focused rebuild replacing worn components (timing chain, bearings, piston rings) without full block machining.
types.frontDisclaimer: Lower cost than recon; not for severely damaged blocks.
types.backDescription: A rebuilt Evoque engine targets specific failure points while retaining reusable components where viable - balancing cost and long-term reliability for typical wear patterns.
backBullets: DW12 units typically need turbo and injector rebuilds at 100k miles
PT204 petrol rebuilds focus on high-pressure pump and timing chain
Shorter lead time (3-7 days) than full reconditioning
types.cta: Get rebuilt Range Rover Evoque engine quotes

types.title: Range Rover Evoque Used Engines
types.description: Low-mileage salvage unit tested for compression and oil pressure before despatch.
types.frontDescription: Low-mileage salvage unit tested for compression and oil pressure before despatch.
types.frontDisclaimer: Fitment advised within 14 days of delivery.
types.backDescription: A used Evoque engine is the fastest budget option but carries higher risk if service history is unknown - only buy from breakers offering compression test results.
backBullets: Match your engine code exactly: AJ200D, 204DTA, DW12, PT204
2015-2019 Ingenium diesel used units often have latent timing chain wear
Maximum 60-day warranty typical; verify before purchase
types.cta: Check used Range Rover Evoque engine availability

types.title: Range Rover Evoque Remanufactured Engines
types.description: Factory-authorised rebuild with new cylinder liners, crankshaft grind, complete gasket set and camshafts.
types.frontDescription: Factory-authorised rebuild with new cylinder liners, crankshaft grind, complete gasket set and camshafts.
types.frontDisclaimer: Premium option - includes all-new wearable components.
types.backDescription: A remanufactured Evoque engine meets or exceeds original factory tolerances with every component inspected or replaced - the closest you can get to a new engine.
backBullets: Every AJ20D4 and PT204 unit receives upgraded DPF and injector calibrations
Recommended for owners planning to keep their Evoque for 3+ years
types.cta: Compare remanufactured Range Rover Evoque engine costs

types.title: Range Rover Evoque Refurbished Engines
types.description: Cleaned, tested and serviced used engine with bolt-on parts replaced where visibly worn.
types.frontDescription: Cleaned, tested and serviced used engine with bolt-on parts replaced where visibly worn.
types.frontDisclaimer: Entry-level option; timing chain not always replaced.
types.backDescription: A refurbished Evoque engine sits between used and rebuilt - cosmetic and serviceable items are addressed, but internal components are not automatically upgraded.
backBullets: Suitable for 2011-2015 DW12 engines with documented oil change history
Not recommended for high-mileage Ingenium diesels with oil dilution history
30-90 day warranty; confirm timing chain condition before fitting

engineSizes.intro: Every displacement factory-fitted to the Range Rover Evoque in the UK market is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes for each variant.

groups.title: Range Rover Evoque Diesel Engine Sizes

items.title: Range Rover Evoque 2.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.2-litre PSA DW12 diesel powered the first-generation Evoque (L538, 2011-2015) across three power outputs - eD4, TD4 and SD4. This Ford-sourced unit uses a twin-turbo configuration in SD4 form and common-rail direct injection. Known for robust bottom-end strength, these engines develop peak torque low in the rev range (380-420Nm from 1750rpm) and were offered with both manual and automatic transmissions.
commonFailurePoints: Turbocharger VGT vane sticking from soot buildup (90k+ miles)
timing chain stretch on high-mileage units
items.cta: Get quotes for Range Rover Evoque 2.2L diesel engines

items.title: Range Rover Evoque 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The Ingenium AJ200D family launched in 2015, replacing the PSA 2.2-litre unit with a modular all-aluminium design featuring low-friction coatings and variable-geometry turbocharging. These Euro 6 engines powered the facelift L538 (2015-2019) and continued into the second-generation L551 (2019-present) with mild-hybrid assistance on D200 variants. The 2.0-litre Ingenium diesel accounts for the majority of UK replacement enquiries due to oil dilution and timing chain failures.
commonFailurePoints: Oil dilution from incomplete DPF regeneration causing timing chain tensioner failure
items.cta: Get quotes for Range Rover Evoque 2.0L diesel engines

groups.title: Range Rover Evoque Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Range Rover Evoque 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre Si4 petrol engine (PT204) powered first-generation L538 Evoques (2011-2015) with 237hp, sharing architecture with Ford’s Ecoboost family. After a hiatus, the Ingenium-branded 2.0-litre petrol returned in 2017 (P250, 247hp) and continued into the second-generation L551 (2019-present) with mild-hybrid technology on P200 and P250 variants. This all-aluminium turbocharged four-cylinder uses direct injection and twin-scroll turbocharging.
commonFailurePoints: High-pressure fuel pump failure causing fuel system contamination
timing chain stretch at higher mileage
items.cta: Compare Range Rover Evoque 2.0L petrol engine prices

groups.title: Range Rover Evoque Hybrid / MHEV Engine Sizes

items.title: Range Rover Evoque 1.5L Hybrid Engines
items.description: The P300e plug-in hybrid combined a 1.5-litre three-cylinder Ingenium petrol engine (AJ20P4H) with an 80kW electric motor, producing 305hp combined and offering up to 51 miles of electric-only range. Fitted to second-generation L551 Evoques from 2020 to 2024, this is the smallest displacement engine ever offered in the Evoque. The petrol unit is transversely mounted while the electric motor drives the rear axle, creating an electric rear drive (eRAD) system for all-wheel-drive capability without a physical propshaft.
commonFailurePoints: Inverter failure from water ingress or thermal stress
items.cta: Enquire about Range Rover Evoque 1.5L hybrid engine availability

items.title: Range Rover Evoque 2.0L MHEV Engines
items.description: The second-generation L551 Evoque introduced mild-hybrid technology across both diesel and petrol 2.0-litre Ingenium engines. A belt-integrated starter generator (BiSG) harvests energy during deceleration, restarts the engine seamlessly and provides torque assist, improving fuel economy by approximately 10-15%. The system operates at 48V and includes a small lithium-ion battery under the floor.
commonFailurePoints: timing chain tensioner issues on early 2019 builds
items.cta: Enquire about Range Rover Evoque 2.0L MHEV engine availability

items.title: Range Rover Evoque Diesel Engines
items.description: Diesel powered the majority of Evoque sales across two generations, from the robust PSA DW12 (2011-2015) to the problematic but efficient Ingenium AJ200D family (2015-present) which dominates UK replacement enquiries.
items.descriptor: Diesel powered the majority of Evoque sales across two generations, from the robust PSA DW12 (2011-2015) to the problematic but efficient Ingenium AJ200D family (2015-present) which dominates UK replacement enquiries.
items.familiesLabel: Common Range Rover Evoque Diesel Engine Families

AJ200D / 204DTA - First-generation Ingenium 2.0-litre, known for oil dilution and timing chain failure (2015-2019)
AJ20D4 - Second-generation Ingenium 2.0-litre with mild hybrid option on D200 (2019-present)
foundIn: First Generation Facelift (L538, 2015-2019) - AJ200D Ingenium 2.0-litre (TD4, SD4)
Second Generation (L551, 2019-present) - AJ20D4 Ingenium 2.0-litre (D165, D200 MHEV)
knownFor: Strong motorway fuel economy (45-55mpg real-world on DW12 units)
Oil dilution from incomplete DPF regeneration on 2015-2019 Ingenium engines
Timing chain tensioner failure requiring full rebuild on AJ200D family
High UK replacement demand on 2015-2019 TD4 and SD4 variants

items.title: Range Rover Evoque Petrol Engines
items.description: Petrol Evoques are less common than diesel but offer smoother performance and fewer catastrophic failures, spanning the Si4 (2011-2015) and later Ingenium P200/P250 units with mild-hybrid assistance from 2019.
items.descriptor: Petrol Evoques are less common than diesel but offer smoother performance and fewer catastrophic failures, spanning the Si4 (2011-2015) and later Ingenium P200/P250 units with mild-hybrid assistance from 2019.
items.familiesLabel: Common Range Rover Evoque Petrol Engine Families
families: PT204 MHEV / AJ20P4 - Second-generation 2.0-litre with 48V mild hybrid (2019-present)
knownFor: More reliable than Ingenium diesel equivalents with fewer systemic failures
High-pressure fuel pump failure causing fuel system contamination
Turbo wastegate rattle on higher-mileage Si4 units (80k+ miles)
Lower UK replacement volume but growing as diesels fall from favour
typicalModels: Si4 · P250 Ingenium · P200 · P250 MHEV

items.title: Range Rover Evoque Mild Hybrid (MHEV) Engines
items.description: Second-generation L551 Evoques (2019-present) introduced belt-integrated starter generator (BiSG) 48V mild hybrid systems on both diesel D200 and petrol P200/P250 variants.
items.descriptor: Second-generation L551 Evoques (2019-present) introduced belt-integrated starter generator (BiSG) 48V mild hybrid systems on both diesel D200 and petrol P200/P250 variants.
items.familiesLabel: Range Rover Evoque MHEV Engine Codes
families: 48V belt-integrated starter generator recovers energy during braking and coasting
Provides torque assist under acceleration and seamless stop-start restart
Mild hybrid cannot drive on electric power alone - petrol/diesel engine always engaged
importantNotes: MHEV engine replacement requires 48V battery inspection and hybrid system reset
Replacement costs are £200-400 higher than non-MHEV equivalents due to additional components
BiSG unit failure can trigger dashboard warnings but does not always require full engine replacement
Availability is more limited than standard engines - confirm MHEV compatibility before ordering
items.cta: Enquire about Range Rover Evoque MHEV engine availability

items.title: Range Rover Evoque Plug-in Hybrid (PHEV) Engines
items.description: The P300e (2020-2024) combined a 1.5-litre three-cylinder Ingenium petrol engine with an 80kW electric motor driving the rear axle, creating electric all-wheel drive without a physical propshaft.
items.descriptor: The P300e (2020-2024) combined a 1.5-litre three-cylinder Ingenium petrol engine with an 80kW electric motor driving the rear axle, creating electric all-wheel drive without a physical propshaft.
items.familiesLabel: Range Rover Evoque PHEV Engine Codes
families: AJ20P4H / AJ20-P3 - 1.5-litre three-cylinder petrol PHEV (147hp engine + 227hp electric motor)
Electric motor powers the rear wheels independently (eRAD - electric rear axle drive)
Up to 51 miles of electric-only range from 15kWh battery
Petrol engine drives front wheels and acts as generator when battery depletes
foundIn: P300e · P300e R-Dynamic · P300e HSE Dynamic (L551, 2020-2024)
importantNotes: P300e engine replacement is complex - requires high-voltage system disconnection by certified technicians
Inverter failure (water ingress/thermal stress) is the most common hybrid-specific fault
Battery degradation may require separate replacement; not covered by engine warranties
Fewer specialist suppliers handle PHEV engine swaps - confirm expertise before committing

engineYears.h2: Range Rover Evoque Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: From the 2011 launch of the first-generation L538 to the current L551 mild-hybrid and plug-in hybrid range, this timeline covers every UK-market engine change across the Range Rover Evoque’s production run.

years.description: The original Evoque launched with Ford/PSA-sourced four-cylinder engines - the 2.2-litre DW12 diesel and 2.0-litre Si4 petrol.
keyChanges: Five-door and three-door (Coupé) body styles launched simultaneously
Front-wheel drive eD4 diesel introduced for lower CO2 emissions
Six-speed automatic transmission available on SD4 and Si4; replaced by nine-speed ZF unit from 2014
Active Driveline part-time 4WD optional on all-wheel drive variants
popularModels: Range Rover Evoque eD4 · Range Rover Evoque TD4 · Range Rover Evoque SD4 · Range Rover Evoque Si4
knownFor: Robust PSA DW12 diesel with good high-mileage reliability (150k+ miles achievable)
Si4 petrol is smooth but thirsty - real-world 25-28mpg
Turbocharger VGT coking on high-mileage DW12 units (90k+ miles)
Evoque set the template for luxury compact SUVs - huge commercial success in the UK

eD4 front-wheel drive variant continues with lower-output Ingenium (204DTD)

faq.h2: Range Rover Evoque Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Range Rover Evoque engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A replacement engine for a Range Rover Evoque typically costs between £1,800 and £5,500 supply-only, depending on the engine code, variant and condition type. Used engines start around £800, while rebuilt and reconditioned units range from £1,500 to £5,500. Supply and fit adds £400-£800.
keyPoints: Used 2.2 DW12 diesel engines: £800-£2,500 depending on mileage and warranty
Rebuilt 2.0 Ingenium diesel (AJ200D): £1,800-£4,200 with 12-month warranty
Second-generation D165/D200 MHEV engines: £2,000-£4,800 supply only
Main dealer replacement can exceed £7,500-£10,000 - specialists offer significant savings
Always confirm if the price includes upgraded timing chain components
items.cta: Get instant Range Rover Evoque engine replacement quotes from UK specialists

items.question: What are the most common Range Rover Evoque engine problems I should watch for?
items.answer: The Ingenium 2.0 diesel (2015-2019) dominates failure reports with three major issues: timing chain failure from oil dilution, turbocharg failure around 80k-100k miles, and high-pressure fuel pump failure. The older 2.2 DW12 diesel is more robust but suffers turbo VGT coking at high mileage.
keyPoints: Oil dilution from incomplete DPF regeneration thins engine oil and destroys timing chain tensioners
Timing chain rattle on cold start is a red flag - don't ignore it on 2015-2019 Ingenium diesels
Turbo failure symptoms: whining noise, loss of power, blue/black smoke - budget £1,500-£2,000 to replace
HPFP failure on 2.0 petrol engines can contaminate the entire fuel system
2011-2015 2.2 diesel is more reliable but turbo issues appear from 90k miles
items.cta: Diagnose your Evoque engine problem and compare repair costs

items.question: Is the Ingenium diesel engine really that bad? Should I avoid it?
items.answer: Early Ingenium diesels (2015-2017) have significant design flaws around DPF regeneration and timing chain tensioners that caused many premature failures. Later examples (2018-2019 and second-generation L551 from 2019) are much improved, with revised tensioners and updated engine management software.
keyPoints: 2015-2017 Ingenium diesels are the highest-risk - oil dilution failures are well-documented
2018-2019 facelift models received updated timing chain components and tensioners
Second-generation L551 (2019+) D165/D200 MHEV diesels are significantly more reliable
A well-maintained late Ingenium with regular oil changes (every 8k miles, not 21k) can be fine
Many specialists now sell upgraded rebuilds with revised parts that address original failure points
items.cta: Find a rebuilt Ingenium diesel with upgraded timing components for your Evoque

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on my Range Rover Evoque, or should I scrap it?
items.answer: Replacement is financially sensible if your Evoque is worth more than the repair cost and otherwise in good condition. For 2011-2015 models worth £4,000-£7,000, a £1,500-£3,000 used or rebuilt engine makes sense. For 2015-2017 Ingenium diesels with high mileage (100k+), scrapping or selling for spares may be better if replacement costs exceed £4,000.
keyPoints: Get a specialist rebuild quote before deciding - dealer prices are often double specialist rates
A rebuilt engine with 12-month warranty costs £1,800-£4,200 fitted via specialist networks
Factor in your car's condition: bodywork, interior, suspension, transmission all matter
If your Evoque has other major issues (gearbox, electrical faults), scrapping may be better
Selling privately as a "spares or repair" can return £1,500-£3,000 depending on model and mileage
items.cta: Compare rebuilt vs used engine prices for your Evoque - get free quotes

items.question: How long do Range Rover Evoque engines typically last?
items.answer: A well-maintained 2.2 DW12 diesel (2011-2015) can reach 150,000-180,000 miles with proper servicing. The Ingenium 2.0 diesel (2015-2019) has a more variable lifespan - some fail before 60,000 miles due to oil dilution, while well-maintained examples can reach 120,000-150,000 miles. Second-generation MHEV diesels are still proving themselves but appear more robust.
keyPoints: 2.2 DW12 diesel is the most durable - 150k+ miles achievable with regular oil changes
2015-2019 Ingenium diesel lifespan depends entirely on driving patterns and oil change frequency
Frequent short journeys kill Ingenium diesels quickly (DPF regen cycles cause oil dilution)
2.0 petrol engines (Si4, P250) typically last 100k-130k miles before major issues appear
Key to longevity: 8,000-mile oil changes on Ingenium diesels - ignore the 21k service interval
items.cta: Get quotes for a high-mileage replacement engine with upgraded components

items.question: What's the difference between rebuilt, reconditioned and used Evoque engines?
items.answer: Used engines are salvaged from donor vehicles (£800-£2,500) - cheapest but unknown history. Rebuilt engines replace worn components like timing chains and bearings (£1,500-£4,200) but don't fully machine the block. Reconditioned (recon) engines are stripped, machined to factory tolerances and rebuilt with OEM-grade parts (£1,800-£4,500) - the most thorough option.
keyPoints: Used engines: Fastest turnaround (2-5 days) but limited warranty (30-90 days typical)
Rebuilt engines: Focus on specific failure points, good for moderate damage, 12-month warranty
Reconditioned engines: Full strip-down, ultrasonic cleaning, new pistons/rings/bearings, 12-24 month warranty
Remanufactured engines: Factory-authorised process, closest to new, 24-36 month warranty
For Ingenium diesels with oil dilution damage, skip used - invest in rebuilt with upgraded tensioners
items.cta: Compare used, rebuilt and reconditioned Range Rover Evoque engine prices

items.question: Does the Range Rover Evoque have a timing chain or belt, and when should it be replaced?
items.answer: All Evoque engines use timing chains, not belts. The 2.2 DW12 and 2.0 Ingenium families both have chains. While Land Rover claims they're "lifetime" parts, real-world experience shows replacement is often needed between 60,000-100,000 miles on Ingenium diesels due to tensioner failure and oil dilution damage.
keyPoints: Timing chain rattle on cold start is the first warning sign - don't ignore it
On Ingenium diesels, oil dilution accelerates chain and tensioner wear significantly
2.2 DW12 chains last longer (80k-120k miles) but still need eventual replacement
Preventative chain replacement costs £1,200-£1,800 at a specialist - far cheaper than a new engine
If the chain jumps, the engine is interference-fit - valves hit pistons, requiring full replacement
items.cta: Get a fixed-price timing chain replacement quote for your Range Rover Evoque

items.question: What causes oil dilution on Ingenium diesel Evoques, and how do I prevent it?
items.answer: Oil dilution happens when the DPF regeneration cycle injects excess diesel fuel that passes into the engine sump. This thins the oil, destroys its lubricating properties, and causes timing chain tensioner failure and bearing wear. It's most common on cars used for short journeys where the DPF never completes its regeneration cycle.
keyPoints: Frequent short trips (under 20 minutes) prevent DPF regeneration from completing properly
The tell-tale signs: rising oil level on dipstick and a strong diesel smell from the oil filler cap
Prevention: Regular 8,000-mile oil changes (not the 21,000-mile Land Rover schedule)
Prevention: Monthly 30+ minute motorway runs to allow passive DPF regeneration
If you only do short journeys, buy a petrol or PHEV Evoque instead of a diesel
items.cta: Compare oil dilution repair costs and rebuilt engine options for your Evoque

items.question: Which Range Rover Evoque engine is the most reliable?
items.answer: The 2.2-litre DW12 diesel (2011-2015) is the most reliable Evoque engine, with many examples passing 150,000 miles with proper maintenance. Among Ingenium engines, the second-generation D165/D200 MHEV diesel (2019+) is significantly improved over 2015-2018 units. For petrol buyers, the P250 MHEV (2019+) is the most dependable choice.
keyPoints: Best overall: 2.2 DW12 diesel (2011-2015) - robust, well-proven Ford/PSA unit
Best Ingenium diesel: D165/D200 MHEV (2019+) - revised tensioners and software
Best petrol: P250 MHEV (2019+) - smoother and more reliable than early Si4 units
Engines to approach with caution: 2015-2017 Ingenium diesel (AJ200D, 204DTA)
For high-mileage users, the 2011-2015 2.2 diesel still offers best value and longevity
items.cta: Find a replacement engine for the most reliable Evoque variants

items.question: Can I fit a used engine from a different year Evoque into my car?
items.answer: Compatibility depends on matching engine codes, not just model years. The 2.2 DW12 (2011-2015) engines are generally interchangeable across those years. Ingenium engines have more complexity - early 2015-2017 AJ200D units differ from later 2018-2019 revisions. Second-generation L551 (2019+) engines are not backwards compatible with first-generation L538 models.
keyPoints: Engine code matching is essential - AJ200D, 204DTA, DW12, PT204 must match exactly
2015-2017 Ingenium engines can sometimes fit 2018-2019 cars but need ECU reprogramming
L551 MHEV engines (AJ20D4, AJ20P4) won't fit L538 chassis - different mounts and electronics
Always verify the donor vehicle's year and engine code before purchasing
A specialist supplier can confirm compatibility using your VIN number
items.cta: Get expert advice on Range Rover Evoque engine compatibility - enter your reg
